metadata.description: A storage device (disk / SSD / NVMe) component. deviceClass distinguishes a physical drive (wwn/serial/bay) from a virtual disk presented to a guest (which binds_to its backing Storage resource). Mirrors Redfish Drive (+ SNIA Swordfish at the system level).

deviceClass.description: Device realization (common-elements.md §7). virtual = a guest virtual disk (binds_to its backing volume); passthrough = whole physical disk to a guest.

partitionMechanism.description: Only when deviceClass=partition: e.g. namespace (NVMe) / lun carved from a parent.

location.description: Drive bay / slot within the parent, e.g. Bay 7 (Redfish PhysicalLocation). Primary discriminator.

wwn.description: World-Wide Name — globally unique block-device identity.

role.description: Semantic usage, e.g. boot | data | ceph-osd | cache.

mediaType.description: Media, e.g. hdd, ssd, nvme (Redfish MediaType).

protocol.description: Bus/transport, e.g. sas, sata, nvme (Redfish Protocol).

rotationSpeedRpm.description: Spindle speed for rotational media; 0 / omitted for SSD/NVMe.

devicePath.description: OS device path once Realized, e.g. /dev/disk/by-id/....

relationships.description: The host this drive is installed in; parent carries the reconciled storage rollup (§26).
For deviceClass=virtual/passthrough, the guest the disk is presented to.
For a virtual disk, the backing storage resource it is provisioned from.
parentDevice — the physical disk a passthrough/partition (e.g. NVMe namespace) is derived from (common-elements §7).

adopts.role: drive component vocabulary (capacity, media, protocol, wwn, serial, bay)
storage-device modeling alignment at the system level
